migration_inventory.inventory_rule: Every physical table introduced by a CREATE TABLE statement, including partitions and installation-internal tables, appears in tables exactly once. A migration that adds or removes a CREATE TABLE statement must update this contract.

legacy_role_mapping.binding_scope: default organization and all of its project/environment descendants

user_state.active: create an active default-organization membership and mapped organization role binding
user_state.inactive: create a disabled default-organization membership and mapped disabled role binding; do not authorize an existing session

invitation_state.pending_unexpired: preserve the bearer digest, expiry, inviter, and mapped target role in the default organization
invitation_state.accepted_or_expired: retain only the existing lifecycle/evidence state; never mint a new bearer

legacy_role_mapping.oidc_state: Map every non-null default, email, and group role value through roles; an unknown value aborts verification.

default_scope_contract.unknown_setting_key_behavior: abort migration verification; never guess or copy an unregistered key

scope_authorities.installation: Deployment and operator boundary; never an implicit tenant.
scope_authorities.global_identity: Installation-global identity with organization access only through memberships.
scope_authorities.organization: Security, identity, billing, residency, and provider-credential boundary.
scope_authorities.project: Ownership, application administration, and cost-allocation boundary inside one organization.
scope_authorities.environment: Independent runtime publication boundary inside one project.
scope_authorities.action_scope: The explicit installation/organization/project/environment scope of an audited or idempotent action.
scope_authorities.event_scope: Immutable organization/project/environment attribution copied when an operational fact is created.
scope_authorities.installation_evidence: Operator-only evidence that cannot safely be attributed to one tenant; it is not exposed through tenant APIs.

deletion.installation_root: No in-place deletion. Destroying an installation is an operator-controlled deployment and data-destruction procedure.
deletion.global_identity_guarded: Deactivate first. Physical deletion requires no live memberships, sessions, identity links, or retained legal/security dependency; historical actor IDs are tombstoned rather than rewritten.
deletion.scope_parent_async: Mark deleting, revoke admission and credentials, drain work, apply retention, then delete unpublished children. Retained facts and immutable history keep scope IDs/tombstones.
deletion.configuration_restrict: Disable first. Restrict physical deletion while any grant, active revision, runtime, fact, pricing provenance, or audit evidence references the row.
deletion.unpublished_child_cascade: May cascade only with an unpublished parent. Once published or referenced by evidence, retain/restrict according to the parent history policy.
deletion.immutable_history: Append-only after publication. Do not physically delete while referenced by a runtime, fact, rollback candidate, audit record, or supported recovery point.
deletion.credential_revoke: Revoke immediately for new authentication/use. Retain encrypted/digested revision metadata while runtime, audit, or recovery evidence references it; never retain plaintext.
deletion.ephemeral_expiry: Delete only after the explicit expiry/replay horizon; expiry must not erase required audit evidence.
deletion.fact_retention: Immutable after creation except bounded reconciliation state. Delete only through scoped retention after required aggregation/evidence preservation.
deletion.installation_internal_retention: Not tenant-mutable. Delete only through the documented operator maintenance/replay policy.
deletion.physical_partition: Lifecycle is owned by the partitioned parent; never delete or expose it as an independent resource.
deletion.action_scoped: Deletion follows the action's resolved scope and record class; tenant deletion cannot erase installation audit or another scope's evidence.

transfer.forbidden: Ownership transfer across the authoritative boundary is not supported in beta. Clone/export, validate, activate, and delete the source.
transfer.inherited: No independent transfer; the row remains with its parent.
transfer.global_identity_membership_only: The global identity is not transferred. Access changes by adding or removing organization memberships and bindings.
transfer.grant_only: The owned resource does not move. Explicit grants may be created or revoked inside the same organization.
transfer.not_applicable: Installation-internal state or immutable facts cannot be transferred.
transfer.action_scope_fixed: The action/fact keeps the scope captured at creation and cannot be reassigned.

retention.installation_lifetime: Retain for the installation lifetime and through full restore.
retention.identity_lifetime: Retain while the identity or any membership/link is active, then under security/privacy policy with tombstoned historical actor references.
retention.configuration_history: Retain active and rollback-relevant revisions plus all versions referenced by runtime, fact, audit, export, or supported backup evidence.
retention.credential_history: Retain encrypted/digested revisions while referenced; plaintext is never durable. Revocation metadata is immutable evidence.
retention.session_expiry: Delete after session expiry or revocation once required audit evidence is emitted.
retention.invitation_expiry: Delete unaccepted invitations after expiry; retain acceptance/revocation as audit evidence rather than bearer material.
retention.idempotency_expiry: Delete an idempotency replay record only after its operation-specific replay window expires and any required audit evidence is emitted.
retention.authorization_flow_expiry: Delete encrypted/opaque flow state after its short explicit expiry.
retention.public_auth_window: Delete opaque admission buckets after their rolling window plus bounded skew.
retention.request_metadata: Default 30 days, bounded 1-3650 days, selected by the owning organization/environment policy and captured with the record.
retention.usage_fact: Default 90 days, bounded 1-3650 days, then aggregate without losing incompleteness, price provenance, or scope.
retention.usage_aggregate: Retain scoped aggregates until an explicit organization retention/export policy permits deletion; current 2.x behavior has no automatic hourly deletion.
retention.audit_evidence: Default 365 days, bounded 1-3650 days, subject to organization policy and installation security/legal minimums.
retention.media_job: After terminal deletion, retain metadata for the request-retention period; media bytes follow the separate durable-media lifecycle and are never stored here.
retention.outbox: Retain unpublished rows; retain published rows at least seven days before maintenance deletion.
retention.usage_replay_receipt: Retain for the seven-day replay horizon plus five-minute clock-skew grace.
retention.usage_epoch: Retain unresolved epochs indefinitely; delete gracefully closed or acknowledged epochs after usage retention.
retention.usage_gap: Retain raw gaps for usage retention, then roll into scoped or installation-only hourly evidence.
retention.singleton_state: Retain current installation coordination state; replacement is transactional and historical changes are audited where security-relevant.
retention.pricing_history: Retain every revision referenced by usage/runtime evidence and every supported export/recovery point.
retention.physical_partition: Same retention as the parent table.

reference.root: Root installation record; all scoped hierarchy ultimately belongs to this installation.
reference.global_identity: May reference installation-global users, but authorization requires a current membership/binding; creator/actor references never grant access.
reference.same_organization_composite: Composite foreign keys must prove organization ancestry for parent and child. Cross-organization references are forbidden.
reference.same_project_composite: Composite foreign keys must prove organization and project ancestry for parent and child.
reference.same_environment_composite: Composite foreign keys must prove organization, project, and environment ancestry for parent and child.
reference.provider_grant_required: An environment may reference an organization provider/revision only through an active same-organization project/environment grant compiled into its runtime.
reference.credential_lookup: The opaque lookup ID is installation-unique only for pre-scope lookup. The resolved credential row proves one environment and reveals no scope before successful authentication.
reference.event_scope_immutable: Copy and constrain organization/project/environment at creation. Reads, rollups, and exports never infer scope from mutable resources.
reference.action_scope_explicit: Store an explicit scope kind and IDs. Polymorphic resource text is display metadata, not an authorization edge.
reference.installation_internal: No tenant API reference. If affected scope is known, emit a separately constrained scoped fact rather than exposing global state.
reference.physical_partition: Inherits schema, scope, and integrity from its partitioned parent.
reference.pricing_same_organization: Provider-specific prices and price revisions must reference providers in the same organization; environments pin immutable pricing references.
reference.request_fact_composite: Composite request ID, timestamp, and full scope must match the parent request/anchor and all referenced scoped dimensions.

default_scope_migration.installation_identity: Add and persist installation.id; derive and persist versioned default organization/project/environment IDs without changing existing identifiers.
default_scope_migration.remain_global: Keep installation/global authority; create memberships or scoped edges where required, without duplicating identity IDs.
default_scope_migration.attach_default_organization: Backfill the deterministic default organization and preserve the existing row ID/revision.
default_scope_migration.attach_default_project: Backfill the deterministic default project and preserve the existing row ID/revision.
default_scope_migration.attach_default_environment: Backfill full default organization/project/environment ancestry and preserve the existing row ID/revision.
default_scope_migration.inherit_parent: Backfill scope from an integrity-checked parent relationship; disagreement or orphaning fails verification.
default_scope_migration.derive_request_scope: Backfill immutable scope from the request/anchor and verify every related credential, route, provider, and runtime belongs to it.
default_scope_migration.derive_action_scope: Backfill from the typed action/resource relationship. Ambiguous polymorphic rows remain installation-only evidence; never guess by name.
default_scope_migration.split_settings: Classify each allowlisted key as installation, organization, project, or environment; copy the current key only to its specified default authority.
default_scope_migration.create_default_provider_grants: Attach provider ownership to the default organization and create explicit grants to the default project/environment before compiling an environment runtime.
default_scope_migration.split_usage_evidence: Retain process-wide unknown-loss evidence as installation-only and create environment-scoped evidence only where immutable attribution is provable.
default_scope_migration.not_applicable_new_resource: No 2.x row exists. Create the resource only through an explicitly scoped post-migration API after its feature gate is enabled.
default_scope_migration.physical_partition: Inherits the requests backfill; partition rows are not independently migrated.

uniqueness: phase + precedence + exact resource scope + policy-set ID

planned_resources.storage_disposition: non-durable immutable projection embedded in one environment runtime; never an independent table or mutation API

contract_invariants: No durable resource is implicitly global. Installation/global rows have an explicit rationale and no tenant API enumeration surface.
UUID possession never authorizes access; application authorization and composite database integrity both enforce ancestry.
RLS may add defense in depth but is not the sole isolation boundary.
Provider use crosses organization-to-environment ownership only through an explicit same-organization grant.
Provider credential and grant invalidations enter credential authority before a cached environment runtime may admit new work.
Operational and financial facts copy immutable scope at creation; rollups and exports never infer it from mutable configuration.
Public credential lookup IDs are installation-unique for pre-scope lookup but disclose no scope and cannot select a tenant.
Default-scope migration preserves existing IDs, revisions, secrets, route slugs, runtime evidence, and usage totals and aborts on ambiguous ancestry.
Cross-owner transfer is unsupported in beta; clone/export and reactivation preserve evidence boundaries.
Deletion cannot erase another scope's data or evidence and cannot make retained facts change ownership.
Durable records remain metadata-only; unknown usage or price remains incomplete or unpriced, never zero.
